_README: KPI dataset for the Reach Riverside logic model — BASELINE + TIME-SERIES + GOAL format. Keyed by EXACT logic-model row label (Logic Model Alignment from the REACH metrics dictionary). Each indicator carries: 'baseline' (2018 benchmark), 'series' (2023/2024/2025 actuals), and 'target' (2026 goal). Values may be a number or a string like 'tbd' / 'did not track' (treated as missing). 'direction' = 'up' (higher is better) or 'down' (lower is better). The visualization shows PROGRESS from the 2018 baseline toward the 2026 goal, and the status color reflects how far along that progress is. VALUES are real figures pulled from the REACH metrics dictionary (Google Sheet); indicator→outcome mapping uses the dictionary's Logic Model Alignment column.

Increased acreage RESTORED.definition: Accumulated acreage of property 'restored' or converted to communal use through the RESTORE initiative.

Increased access to job skills training.indicator: Individuals with access to job-skill training
Increased access to job skills training.definition: Individuals participating in EMPOWER (KCC) or RISE (TWH) — building technical skills necessary for employment.

Increased employment and job retention.definition: Workforce-program participants (EMPOWER/RISE) who secured jobs providing sufficient income for financial stability and asset accumulation.

Parents/guardians committed to family de.definition: Parents/caregivers actively engaging in family development — setting family goals, Family Partnership Agreements, working toward identified objectives. 10-year goal is 80%.

Individuals engaged in civic life and co.indicator: Imani Village residents engaged in civic & community life
Individuals engaged in civic life and co.definition: Households in Imani Village rental units participating in civic activities and community engagement (community meetings, Tenant Council, volunteer opportunities). 10-year goal is 30%.

Increased financial investment in commun.definition: Total Development Cost (hard + soft) invested in physical infrastructure and development projects through RESTORE and other REACH initiatives during the period.

Increased wages.definition: Families in the target area (Census Tract 30.02) whose household income exceeds 60% of the Area Median Income for the City of Wilmington — indicating economic stability beyond deeply subsidized housing.
